examples.title: Frame Representation Structure
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how frames can be represented with slots, values, and inheritance mechanisms
examples.code:
CLASS Frame:
 name: string
 slots: dictionary
 parent_frames: list

 FUNCTION get_slot(slot_name):
 IF slots.contains(slot_name):
 RETURN slots[slot_name]
 ELSE IF parent_frames is not empty:
 FOR each parent IN parent_frames:
 value = parent.get_slot(slot_name)
 IF value != null:
 RETURN value
 RETURN null

 FUNCTION add_slot(slot_name, value, is_default=false):
 slots[slot_name] = Slot(value, is_default)

 FUNCTION inherit_from(parent_frame):
 parent_frames.add(parent_frame)

// Example: Creating university domain frames
CREATE Frame PERSON
ADD_SLOT(PERSON, "name", "")
ADD_SLOT(PERSON, "age", 0)

CREATE Frame STUDENT with PARENT PERSON
ADD_SLOT(STUDENT, "student_id", "")
ADD_SLOT(STUDENT, "gpa", 0.0, is_default=true)

CREATE student_alice
SET student_alice.parent_frames = [STUDENT]
SET student_alice.slots["name"] = "Alice"
SET student_alice.slots["student_id"] = "S12345"
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the structure of frame-based representation with inheritance. When a slot is queried, the system checks the instance first, then searches parent frames (inheritance chain). Default values can be overridden by specific instances.
examples.timeComplexity: O(d) where d is depth of inheritance hierarchy for slot lookup
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n × s) where n is number of frames and s is average slots per frame

examples.title: Semantic Network Query Engine
examples.description: A simple query system for semantic networks that traverses nodes and follows relationships
examples.code:
CLASS SemanticNetwork:
 nodes: set
 edges: dictionary // maps (source, label) to set of targets

 FUNCTION add_node(node):
 nodes.add(node)

 FUNCTION add_edge(source, label, target):
 IF edges.contains((source, label)):
 edges[(source, label)].add(target)
 ELSE:
 edges[(source, label)] = {target}

 FUNCTION find_related(source, label):
 RETURN edges.get((source, label), {})

 FUNCTION find_all_paths(start, goal, path=[]):
 path = path + [start]
 IF start == goal:
 RETURN [path]
 paths = []
 FOR each (source, label) IN edges.keys():
 IF source == start:
 FOR each target IN edges[(source, label)]:
 IF target not in path:
 new_paths = find_all_paths(target, goal, path)
 paths.extend(new_paths)
 RETURN paths

// Example: Building family semantic network
CREATE network
ADD_NODE(network, "John")
ADD_NODE(network, "Mary")
ADD_NODE(network, "David")

ADD_EDGE(network, "John", "married_to", "Mary")
ADD_EDGE(network, "John", "father_of", "David")
ADD_EDGE(network, "Mary", "mother_of", "David")

// Query: Who is David's father?
father = find_related(network, "David", "father_of")
PRINT father // Output: {John}
examples.explanation: This code implements a basic semantic network with nodes and labeled edges. It supports adding relationships and querying for related concepts. The find_all_paths function demonstrates graph traversal for complex relationship finding.
examples.timeComplexity: O(V + E) for basic queries, O(V!) for finding all paths in worst case
examples.spaceComplexity: O(V + E) for storing the network structure

examples.title: Hybrid Rule-Frame System for Diagnosis
examples.description: A hybrid system combining frame-based data structures with logical rules for inference
examples.code:
CLASS HybridDiagnosisSystem:
 frame_base: FrameBase // Stores concepts as frames
 rule_base: RuleBase // Stores inference rules

 FUNCTION diagnose(symptoms):
 // Step 1: Create patient frame from symptoms
 patient = CREATE_FRAME("Patient")
 FOR each symptom IN symptoms:
 ADD_SLOT(patient, symptom, true)
 
 // Step 2: Apply rules to frame data
 candidate_diseases = []
 FOR each rule IN rule_base.rules:
 IF rule.evaluate(patient.frame_data):
 candidate_diseases.add(rule.conclusion)
 
 // Step 3: Rank by confidence using frame inheritance
 RETURN rank_by_confidence(candidate_diseases)

// Define diagnostic rules
RULE flu_symptoms:
 IF patient.has_fever == true
 AND patient.has_body_ache == true
 AND patient.has_cough == true
 THEN confidence(flu) = 0.85

RULE cold_symptoms:
 IF patient.has_runny_nose == true
 AND patient.has_sore_throat == true
 THEN confidence(cold) = 0.70

// Define disease frames for inheritance
FRAME Disease:
 slots: [name, treatment, severity]

FRAME Flu INHERITS Disease:
 slots: [name="Influenza", treatment="Rest and fluids", severity="moderate"]
 defaults: [recovery_days=7]

FRAME Cold INHERITS Disease:
 slots: [name="Common Cold", treatment="OTC medication", severity="mild"]
 defaults: [recovery_days=5]

// Usage
system = HybridDiagnosisSystem()
ADD_RULES(system, [flu_symptoms, cold_symptoms])
ADD_FRAMES(system, [Flu, Cold])

patient_symptoms = ["fever", "body_ache", "cough"]
diagnosis = system.diagnine(patient_symptoms)
PRINT diagnosis // Output: Flu (confidence: 0.85)
examples.explanation: This hybrid system combines frame-based representations (for storing disease concepts and patient data) with rule-based inference (for diagnostic reasoning). Frames provide structured data with inheritance, while rules enable logical deduction from symptoms to diseases.
examples.timeComplexity: O(r × f) where r is number of rules and f is frame evaluation cost
examples.spaceComplexity: O(F + R) where F is frame base size and R is rule base size
